articles.headline: RBI declares record ₹2.11 lakh crore dividend for central government
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India board has approved a record-high surplus transfer of ₹2.11 lakh crore to the Union government for the fiscal year 2023-24. This is more than double the previous year's transfer and significantly exceeds the government's budget estimate of ₹1.02 lakh crore. The move is expected to provide a major boost to the government's fiscal position.

articles.headline: Sensex and Nifty hit lifetime highs after RBI dividend news
articles.summary: Indian equity benchmark indices reached new all-time highs on Wednesday. Investor sentiment was bolstered by the RBI's massive dividend announcement and positive global cues. The BSE Sensex crossed the 75,000 mark during intra-day trade, led by gains in banking, financial services, and metal stocks, reflecting strong domestic liquidity and macroeconomic stability.

articles.headline: Fifth phase of Lok Sabha polling sees 62.2 percent turnout
articles.summary: The fifth phase of India's general elections, covering 49 constituencies across eight states and Union Territories, concluded with a provisional voter turnout of 62.2%. Major urban centers like Mumbai recorded moderate participation. The Election Commission of India noted that the polling process remained largely peaceful with high rural engagement reported in Bihar and Odisha.

articles.headline: BHEL reports 25 percent drop in net profit for Q4
articles.summary: Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL) reported a 25.6% decline in its consolidated net profit to ₹489.62 crore for the fourth quarter ended March 2024. The state-run engineering firm had posted a profit of ₹658 crore in the same period last year. Despite the profit dip, total income rose slightly to ₹8,416.84 crore, though higher raw material costs weighed on the bottom line.

articles.headline: IMF maintains India's growth forecast at 6.8 percent for 2024
articles.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has kept India's economic growth forecast unchanged at 6.8% for the year 2024, citing strong domestic demand and a rising working-age population. The agency noted that India remains the world's fastest-growing major economy. It also recommended that the government continue its focus on fiscal consolidation and infrastructure development.

articles.headline: Air India Express to merge with AIX Connect by October
articles.summary: The Tata Group has announced that the legal merger of its low-cost carriers, Air India Express and AIX Connect (formerly AirAsia India), is on track to be completed by October 2024. The consolidation aims to create a single low-cost entity under the Air India brand. The airline is currently synchronizing its flight codes, crew scheduling, and operational procedures.

articles.headline: Adani Power to acquire Lanco Amarkantak Power for ₹4,101 crore
articles.summary: Adani Power has received approval from the National Company Law Tribunal to acquire the debt-ridden Lanco Amarkantak Power for ₹4,101 crore. This acquisition will add 600 MW of thermal power capacity to Adani Power's portfolio, with potential for further expansion. The move is part of the group's strategy to consolidate its position in India's private thermal power sector.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes new norms to curb front-running in mutual funds
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has proposed a new institutional mechanism to detect and prevent front-running and insider trading within mutual fund houses. The proposal includes mandatory whistle-blower policies and enhanced internal surveillance systems. Asset management companies will be held responsible for identifying suspicious trading patterns by employees.

articles.headline: Centre plans to sell 5 percent stake in Garden Reach Shipbuilders
articles.summary: The Union government is considering a plan to sell a 5% stake in Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers (GRSE) through an Offer for Sale (OFS). The divestment is part of the government's strategy to meet its non-tax revenue targets for the current fiscal year. GRSE is a premier shipyard under the Ministry of Defence with a strong order book for the Indian Navy and Coast Guard.

articles.headline: Iran begins funeral ceremonies for President Ebrahim Raisi
articles.summary: Iran has commenced several days of funeral ceremonies for President Ebrahim Raisi, Foreign Minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian, and others killed in a recent helicopter crash. Thousands gathered in Tabriz and Tehran to pay their respects. Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has declared five days of national mourning and appointed Mohammad Mokhber as acting president.

articles.headline: US markets face major sell-off as AI stocks and yields fluctuate
articles.summary: US markets experienced a breakdown in uncorrelated asset logic as stocks, gold, and crypto fell while bond yields spiked. Triggers included disappointing Broadcom results and large equity issuances by Alphabet to fund AI ambitions. The 10-year Treasury yield rose above 4.5% amid strong jobs data, while escalating tensions in the Middle East added further pressure on global sentiment.

articles.headline: Kotak Mahindra Bank shares recommended for accumulation despite muted FY26
articles.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank appears to have navigated the worst of its margin pressure and asset-quality concerns. While net interest margins may remain under pressure in FY27, improved collection efficiencies in microfinance and credit cards offer support. With the stock trading at a sharp valuation discount of 2.1 times book value, analysts suggest long-term investors accumulate the shares.

articles.headline: Government mandates standard pack sizes for major edible oils
articles.summary: The Department of Consumer Affairs has prescribed nine standard pack sizes for edible oils, ranging from 200ml to 20 litres, to help consumers compare prices easily. Companies have three months to comply with the new rules under the Legal Metrology framework. The move, welcomed by industry bodies, aims to eliminate confusion caused by non-standard packs and improve market transparency.

articles.headline: India must redesign education and research for AI-led future
articles.summary: Industry veterans at The Hindu Huddle argued that India needs to focus on skilling and innovation rather than just job loss fears. Panelists highlighted that less than 10% of board directors understand technology, representing a significant gap in corporate leadership. To create value, India must move beyond being a technology consumer and overhaul its education system for the AI era.

articles.headline: Comprehensive guide to National Pension System for first-time investors
articles.summary: The National Pension System (NPS) has evolved into a low-cost, market-linked retirement solution for all citizens. PFRDA has introduced new features like NPS Vatsalya for minors and the Retirement Income Scheme for phased withdrawals. The article details the structure of Tier-I and Tier-II accounts, investment options including Active and Auto Choice, and tax benefits.

articles.headline: Understanding the RBI swap window for foreign currency deposits
articles.summary: The RBI has announced an FCNR(B) swap window to garner forex inflows and alleviate balance of payment challenges. By bearing the hedging cost for deposits of three to five years, the central bank aims to incentivize banks to attract NRI funds. While a similar measure worked in 2013, the current environment is different due to elevated interest rates in developed markets like the US.

articles.headline: Indian debt mutual funds see renewed interest in lower-rated papers
articles.summary: Exposure to AA and below-rated debt instruments in active debt funds rose from 2.3% to 3.1% over the last year. This calibrated shift follows a long period of de-risking after credit events like the IL&FS collapse. While credit risk funds and medium-duration schemes are cautiously adding exposure for incremental yield, fund managers remain focused on capital protection and liquidity.

articles.headline: ICICI Prudential Multi Sector Passive Fund offers dynamic sector rotation
articles.summary: The ICICI Prudential Multi Sector Passive Fund of Funds uses a structured approach to shift allocations across different sector ETFs based on market cycles and valuations. By dynamically adjusting exposure to cyclicals and defensives, the fund aims to provide consistent alpha while reducing the risk of timing entry into specific themes. The fund has outperformed the Nifty 50 over time.

articles.headline: Motilal Oswal Mutual Fund launches Clean Environment Index Fund
articles.summary: Motilal Oswal Mutual Fund has announced a new fund offer for the 'BSE Clean Environment Index Fund,' which tracks five themes: renewables, electric vehicles, water treatment, recycling, and waste management. The open-ended fund closes for subscription on June 19. It requires a minimum application of ₹500 and features a 1% exit load for redemptions made within 15 days of allotment.

articles.headline: HDFC Mutual Fund restricts lump-sum investments in gold products
articles.summary: HDFC Mutual Fund has temporarily restricted lump-sum subscriptions for its Gold ETF and Gold ETF Fund of Fund effective June 8. For the Gold ETF, subscriptions above ₹25 crore will not be accepted. For the Gold ETF FoF, purchases are limited to ₹10 lakh per PAN per month. The decision was attributed to prevailing economic and market conditions.

articles.headline: US money-market funds reach record holdings amid market volatility
articles.summary: The US money-market industry now holds a record $8.29 trillion as investors seek stability and returns. These funds have become a popular 'parking spot' for everyone from retail savers to corporate treasurers, offering yields near 3.5%. The trend, nicknamed 'T-bill and chill,' reflects a preference for cash-like assets over riskier holdings during periods of interest rate uncertainty.

articles.headline: Cipla poised for recovery as US launch pipeline looks robust
articles.summary: Despite a decline in US revenues during FY26 due to regulatory hurdles and product losses, Cipla is expected to see a gradual recovery. The management is planning significant respiratory and complex generic launches in FY27-28, targeting an exit run-rate of $1 billion for US sales. With a strong net cash position and stable growth in India and Africa, analysts maintain an accumulate call.

articles.headline: Personal health cover essential despite employer-provided group insurance
articles.summary: Employer-provided health insurance often lacks continuity and comprehensive coverage for specific ailments. Experts recommend that employees maintain personal health policies to safeguard against job volatility and cover gaps like OPD consultations and consumables. Additionally, super top-up plans and critical illness riders can enhance financial protection against high medical expenses.

articles.headline: Asset allocation strategy for professionals nearing retirement
articles.summary: A 49-year-old professional with a ₹2.2 crore portfolio is advised to gradually shift equity gains toward fixed income as retirement approaches. To manage market volatility, a 70:30 equity-to-debt allocation is suggested for the next few years, eventually moving toward 60% equity at age 58. This approach aims to protect the retirement corpus while ensuring growth that beats inflation.

articles.headline: BharatPe and YES BANK launch Credit-on-UPI solution BharatPe Flex
articles.summary: BharatPe has introduced 'BharatPe Flex,' a Credit-on-UPI solution developed in partnership with YES BANK. The service allows customers to use a credit line for everyday UPI payments, providing greater flexibility and control over their finances. Users can continue to make payments within the existing UPI ecosystem while accessing integrated credit features.

articles.headline: Shriram General Insurance partners with Piramal Finance for rural access
articles.summary: Shriram General Insurance has entered into a strategic corporate agency partnership with Piramal Finance. The collaboration aims to improve access to insurance solutions across semi-urban and rural markets in India. By leveraging Piramal Finance's network, the partnership intends to expand the reach of general insurance products to underserved customer segments.

articles.headline: Monsoon covers Goa and parts of Karnataka as it advances
articles.summary: The Southwest monsoon has covered Goa and extended into parts of Maharashtra, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, and Tamil Nadu. The IMD predicts favorable conditions for further advancement into the interior peninsula and North-East India over the next three days. However, global climate indicators show a strengthening likelihood of El Nino conditions developing in the tropical Pacific.

articles.headline: Cockroach Janta Party protests in Delhi over examination irregularities
articles.summary: The Cockroach Janta Party held a protest at Jantar Mantar demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged examination system failures. The event saw participation from students and political figures like Sonam Wangchuk and Dipankar Bhattacharya. While the social media hype was high, organizers focused on political accountability for recruitment irregularities.

articles.headline: India's electric bus registrations fall as JBM Auto leads market
articles.summary: Electric bus registrations in India declined 7.2% month-on-month in May, dropping to 322 units due to infrastructure and financing bottlenecks. JBM Auto emerged as the market leader with a 48.8% share after registrations rose to 157 units. Other players like PMI Electro Mobility and Switch Mobility saw declines, highlighting the deployment-led nature of the competitive EV bus market.

articles.headline: TVS Motor to launch premium retail channel TVS Paddock
articles.summary: TVS Motor Company has announced the launch of 'TVS Paddock,' an exclusive retail channel for its premium motorcycle portfolio. Scheduled for launch in Q2 FY27, the network aims to provide immersive brand engagement and expert consultation for enthusiasts. Designed by London-based Checkland Kindleysides, the outlets will focus on a reimagined customer journey from interaction to ownership.

articles.headline: G Square Group revives industrial plot development in Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: Chennai-based real estate developer G Square Group plans to sell industrial plots worth ₹2,000 crore this fiscal year. The company is reviving its industrial development segment after five years, citing support from the state government. In addition to industrial plots, the group aims to sell residential properties worth ₹5,000 crore in Chennai and Coimbatore.

articles.headline: India's nutraceutical industry faces supply chain and distribution challenges
articles.summary: Industry executives warn that India's nutraceutical market, targeting $100 billion by 2030, is struggling with inadequate supply-chain infrastructure. Growing demand in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities is outpacing the development of warehousing and cold-chain logistics. Experts emphasized that brands must focus on distribution depth and consistent reordering rather than just increasing store listings.

articles.headline: Scandinavian Airlines seeks regulatory clearance for India flight operations
articles.summary: Scandinavian Airlines (SAS) is working with Indian authorities to commence flights between Copenhagen and Mumbai after a recent relaunch attempt was turned back for lacking regulatory clearance. The airline expressed commitment to operating in compliance with all requirements and highlighted India as an important market for connecting the Scandinavian and Indian regions.

articles.headline: Pune court allows partition suit in Kalyani family wealth dispute
articles.summary: A Pune court has refused to stay a partition suit filed by Sugandha Hiremath seeking her share of the Kalyani family wealth. The court rejected applications from Gaurishankar Kalyani and his daughter to stay proceedings, ruling that various pending suits were not identical. Hiremath alleges her brothers have been selling and alienating shares in the family's legacy industrial companies.

articles.headline: VOC Port Authority partners with Germany's H2Global for green hydrogen
articles.summary: V.O. Chidambaranar Port Authority in Thoothukudi has signed an MoU with Germany’s H2Global to develop green hydrogen and clean shipping fuel corridors. The partnership will focus on port infrastructure requirements for storage and transport of green ammonia and methanol. The initiative aims to integrate Indian ports into international green fuel supply chains for sustainable shipping.

articles.headline: Google agrees to $30-billion cloud services deal with SpaceX
articles.summary: Alphabet's Google has signed a deal to pay SpaceX $920 million monthly through mid-2029 for computing power to support its AI services. The agreement provides Google access to 110,000 Nvidia chips, representing over 100 megawatts of power. The deal helps Google meet surging demand for its Gemini Enterprise agent platform while expanding its cloud service backlog.

articles.headline: Monsoon arrives in Maharashtra on schedule
articles.summary: The Southwest Monsoon reached Maharashtra on Saturday, June 6, 2026, on schedule. It arrived in Kerala on June 4 and has since advanced into parts of Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Mizoram, Manipur, and Goa. The India Meteorological Department forecasts further advancement in the coming days.

articles.headline: Iran fires missiles, drones at Bahrain, Kuwait; US intercepts
articles.summary: Iran launched ballistic missiles and drones towards Bahrain and Kuwait early Saturday. Bahrain called it a 'serious escalation' and urged Iran to cease attacks. The US military reported intercepting Iranian missiles and drones targeting the Strait of Hormuz and Gulf allies, and struck Iranian surveillance radar sites in response.

articles.headline: Cook arrested for Hauz Rani B&B fire that killed 21
articles.summary: A cook at the Hauz Rani bed-and-breakfast has been arrested in connection with the fire that killed 21 people. The accused claims the fire started after a spark in a fryer. Police are investigating the precise cause, with his lawyer suggesting an electrical short circuit.

articles.headline: Singapore blocks videos targeting Indian diaspora, undermining multiculturalism
articles.summary: Singapore has ordered social media platforms to block content targeting the Indian community, which it says undermines the country's multiculturalism. Police flagged 14 posts promoting a narrative of Indians 'overrunning' Singapore.

articles.headline: Iran's World Cup squad leaves amid US visa row
articles.summary: Iran's World Cup squad departed for Mexico under a diplomatic row after the US denied visas to some support staff. Iran's embassy accused the US of discriminatory treatment, while the US confirmed visas were issued to players and essential staff.

articles.headline: R Praggnanandhaa wins Norway Chess title, first Indian to do so
articles.summary: R Praggnanandhaa secured the Norway Chess title with a 4-match win streak, including victories over Magnus Carlsen and D Gukesh. He became the first Indian to win this prestigious event.

articles.headline: HYDRAA reclaims lands worth ₹1L-cr in Telangana
articles.summary: The Hyderabad Disaster Response and Asset Protection Agency (HYDRAA) has reclaimed government lands worth over ₹1 lakh crore from encroachments. The latest drive protected lands valued at ₹30,000 crore, adding to previous reclaimed assets.

articles.headline: Kerala school students fall ill, nearly 150 affected, 38 hospitalized
articles.summary: Nearly 150 students at an aided primary school in Wayanad, Kerala, fell ill with fever and vomiting. 38 students showing severe symptoms were admitted to the hospital. A holiday has been declared, and samples are being tested.

articles.headline: DMK chief Stalin criticizes TN Govt, functioning due to allies' grace
articles.summary: DMK President M K Stalin stated the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) government in Tamil Nadu is functioning due to the support of DMK and alliance parties. He urged cadres to work towards ending its rule.

articles.headline: Karnataka CM confident of unity despite Congress resignation issue
articles.summary: Karnataka CM DK Shivakumar clarified that the resignation issue of senior Congress leader Ramalinga Reddy is settled. He expressed confidence in party unity and stated that all internal problems have been sorted out.

articles.headline: Auroville uses art to reclaim embattled township from alleged irregularities
articles.summary: Residents of Auroville are holding an exhibition documenting alleged irregularities, including tree cutting and unauthorized construction, they claim have dismantled the community's self-governing structure. They demand restoration of resident governance.

articles.headline: Kerala forms panel to study high-speed railway plan
articles.summary: The Kerala government has formed an expert committee to examine the proposal for a high-speed railway corridor from Thiruvananthapuram to Kannur. The committee will assess technical feasibility, financial viability, and environmental implications.

articles.headline: AP cop dismissed over rape case surrenders
articles.summary: An Andhra Pradesh police inspector, dismissed from service for allegedly raping a woman, has surrendered. He had been absconding after a case was registered against him. The court had dismissed his anticipatory bail plea.

articles.headline: IAF engaged to transport NEET papers: Telangana govt
articles.summary: The Telangana government stated that the Indian Air Force is being engaged to transport NEET question papers for the June 21 exam, ensuring enhanced security and integrity. This reflects the importance placed on the examination's security.

articles.headline: India, Nepal discuss ways to take forward bilateral ties
articles.summary: India and Nepal held discussions on strengthening bilateral ties and development cooperation following the change in government in Nepal. Visiting Nepali FM called for a 'genuinely transformative relationship'.

articles.headline: 9 dead, 18 injured in Punjab road accident
articles.summary: Nine people were killed and 18 injured when a pickup vehicle collided with a truck on the Ferozepur-Fazilka highway in Punjab. Prime Minister Modi announced ex gratia compensation for the deceased and injured.

articles.headline: 5 killed after car rams into dumper truck in Gujarat
articles.summary: Five people died and two were injured when their car collided with a dumper truck on the Morbi-Halvad highway in Gujarat. The accident occurred when the truck was changing lanes.

articles.headline: Protesters block NH-2 in Manipur, seek release of 6 Naga hostages
articles.summary: Protesters blocked the Imphal-Dimapur road in Manipur, demanding the immediate release of six Naga civilians allegedly abducted by Kuki militants. They accused the state and central governments of inaction.

articles.headline: Punjab man held for killing teen, burning body to hide evidence
articles.summary: A 23-year-old man was arrested for allegedly strangling a 16-year-old girl, burning her body, and dumping it in a forest in Punjab. He claims to have been in a relationship with the victim's mother.

articles.headline: MP: Stalker assaults 17-yr-old girl, 2 arrested
articles.summary: A 22-year-old man and his father were arrested in Madhya Pradesh for assaulting a 17-year-old girl. The accused allegedly attacked her with an axe and attempted to strangle her.

articles.headline: UP woman alleges gang rape at gunpoint, probe underway
articles.summary: A 31-year-old woman from Jhansi alleged she was gang-raped at gunpoint in front of her daughter, who was also molested. She claims they were lured into a vehicle while traveling to Kalinjar Fort.

articles.headline: CPI(M) chief slams Rahul Gandhi for BJP-Left nexus charge
articles.summary: CPI(M) General Secretary MA Baby criticized Rahul Gandhi for alleging a nexus between BJP and Left parties, terming it a 'canard'. He stated the CPI(M) has always fought the BJP and will not attend the INDIA bloc meeting due to this issue.

articles.headline: Former TMC MLA, key conspirator in Bhangar bomb blast case, arrested
articles.summary: The NIA arrested former TMC MLA Saokat Molla, who allegedly directed others to make the crude bomb that exploded in Bhangar, West Bengal. One person died and several were injured in the March blast.

articles.headline: Nor'wester kills 1 in Odisha, uproots 200 trees
articles.summary: A severe nor'wester storm hit Odisha's Bhubaneswar, causing one death and uprooting over 200 trees. It also damaged around 300 stalls at an international trade fair and electric poles.

articles.headline: Abhishek Banerjee leaves for Delhi amid wider TMC split fears
articles.summary: TMC national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee traveled to Delhi ahead of the INDIA bloc meeting, amid speculation of a potential split within TMC. His visit, a day before Mamata Banerjee's scheduled trip, fueled political chatter.

articles.headline: Amit Shah directs linking BSF cameras to dist admin in Tripura
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah directed that BSF cameras along the India-Bangladesh border in Tripura be upgraded and linked to district administration, making it the first to implement this CCTV-based security model.

articles.headline: Fire in e-commerce warehouse kills 2
articles.summary: Two employees at an Amazon e-commerce warehouse in Uttarakhand died after a fire erupted late Friday. The cause of the blaze is under investigation. Both deceased were employed by a third-party firm managing the warehouse.

articles.headline: West Bengal govt to probe vandalism during anti-CAA stir
articles.summary: The West Bengal government will form a special cell to investigate vandalism incidents during the 2019 anti-CAA protests. The aim is to recover compensation from those found responsible for damaging public property.

articles.headline: US forces see 1K Hormuz crossings despite Iran attack
articles.summary: US forces recorded nearly 1,000 commercial vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz in the last two months, despite Iran's blockade threats. This figure is higher than private sector estimates.

articles.headline: Kyiv hits Russia after Putin rejection; St Petersburg drone attack
articles.summary: Residents of St Petersburg were told to stay indoors after a large-scale Ukrainian drone attack targeted the city. Russia's Defence Ministry reported shooting down hundreds of drones. This follows Putin's rejection of a meeting with Zelenskyy.

articles.headline: Rosneft CEO: US firms benefit from Hormuz closure
articles.summary: Rosneft CEO Igor Sechin stated that US energy companies were the main beneficiaries of the Strait of Hormuz closure, but warned of strategic risks. He criticized the move as an attempt to reshape global energy markets.

articles.headline: Nearly 500 Ebola cases in central Africa: WHO
articles.summary: Nearly 500 Ebola cases, with 84 deaths, have been confirmed in central Africa, marking a significant increase. The WHO warns the outbreak could become the largest on record if not contained.

articles.headline: US biz pushback led to softer stance on green card policy
articles.summary: Lobbying by US business leaders prompted a softening of the US government's stance on a new green card policy. Concerns were raised about the impact on the tech industry and workforce.

articles.headline: Graphic novel 'Cannon' wins Stella Prize, author hopes for more risks
articles.summary: 'Cannon' by cartoonist Lee Lai won the $60,000 Stella Prize, marking the first time a graphic novel won. Lai hopes this encourages publishers to take more risks with graphic novels.

articles.headline: Jellyfish: Brainless wonders surviving millions of years
articles.summary: Jellyfish have survived for over 500 million years due to their simplicity and efficient nutrient absorption. Their nerve net functions as a brain, and some species have remarkable regenerative abilities.

articles.headline: Anti-romcoms capture love's uneasy realism in modern era
articles.summary: Modern relationships, shaped by greater self-awareness and urgency, are reflected in 'anti-romcoms' like 'The Drama' and 'Beef'. These stories explore love with honesty, discomfort, and often dark twists.

articles.headline: Food apps designed to keep users hooked, study reveals
articles.summary: Food delivery apps are designed to be addictive, leveraging variable reward schedules and psychological triggers. Studies show links to increased consumption of unhealthy food and potential dopamine-driven compulsion.

articles.headline: Privacy: A fragile social contract negotiated over centuries
articles.summary: Privacy, a relatively recent social contract, is constantly negotiated between individuals, governments, and technology. A new book traces its evolution and highlights the challenges in defining and protecting private life in the digital age.

articles.headline: Julian Alvarez: Argentina's X-factor in quest for glory
articles.summary: Julian Alvarez has grown in stature for Argentina, a versatile forward instrumental in their World Cup triumph. With reported interest from Barcelona, his energy, tenacity, and goal-scoring ability make him a key player.

articles.headline: B'desh stuck at border back home, says BSF
articles.summary: BSF officials report that over 1,800 Bangladesh nationals, stuck at the India-Bangladesh border since the lockdown began, have been sent back. Many were held in detention for border crossing attempts. Since April, 597 have been sent back to Bangladesh.

articles.headline: Founder lands, CJP holds its first protest in Delhi
articles.summary: Citizen Justice Party (CJP) founder Sanjeev Kumar launched the party and led its first protest in Delhi demanding 'One Nation, One Education' and a review of the lockdown's economic impact. Kumar claimed widespread support and announced plans for country-wide protests.

articles.headline: Min full like lockdown, but want freedom to move: AIKSCC
articles.summary: The All India Kisan Sangharsh Coordination Committee (AIKSCC) urged PM Modi to allow farmers freedom of movement, similar to the migrant crisis. It highlighted that most farmers support the lockdown but need to sell their produce and access markets.

articles.headline: Before India bloc meet, CPM seeks Cong clarification
articles.summary: Ahead of the India bloc meeting, CPM has asked Congress to clarify its stance on the CPM-led government in Kerala regarding its alliance with Welfare Party. This comes after Congress's alliance for local body elections triggered controversy.

articles.headline: Stay away but stir, Muslims told: Imam
articles.summary: An imam from Kolkata urged Muslims to avoid mosques for Eid prayers due to the pandemic but encouraged them to observe the festival at home. He also condemned any attempts to incite communal violence during this time.

articles.headline: K’taka state board students dominate CET top 10 lists
articles.summary: Karnataka Common Entrance Test (CET) results show state board students securing a majority of top 10 ranks in various streams, outperforming CBSE students. Engineering, Agriculture, and Veterinary Science streams saw state board students leading.

articles.headline: Bihar BJP MLA joins JD(U) 2018 NY party firing case
articles.summary: BJP MLA Raju Singh and his wife have joined JD(U). Singh is an accused in the 2018 New Year's Eve party firing case, which killed a woman. He denied charges, attributing them to political conspiracy.

articles.headline: Reddy row paused for now, as Congress brass steps in
articles.summary: The Congress high command has intervened to de-escalate the conflict between Karnataka Deputy CM DK Shivakumar and minister K Sudhakar over allegations related to land deals. Both leaders have been asked to cease public statements.

articles.headline: Nat’l award winning director Salim Kumar enrolls for MBA
articles.summary: National award-winning Malayalam director Salim Kumar has enrolled in an MBA program at a private university in Thiruvananthapuram. The actor-director, known for his roles in comedies, expressed his desire to enhance his business acumen.

articles.headline: Monsoon knocks on Maha door, but dry-air drag puts it in march inland
articles.summary: Monsoon has arrived in Maharashtra, but its inland progress is slowed by dry winds from the north, delaying the monsoon's arrival in Mumbai. Light rains have occurred in coastal Konkan.

articles.headline: Mirra Magic in Paris
articles.summary: Mirra Andreeva, 17, has advanced to the French Open quarterfinals after defeating Emma Navarro. This is her second Grand Slam quarterfinal, continuing her impressive run in professional tennis.

articles.headline: 535 Raj farmers report adverse impact in exposure to 2 yrs
articles.summary: A survey of 535 farmers in Rajasthan found that 95% reported adverse impacts on their health due to continuous sun exposure over two years. Common issues include skin problems, dehydration, and heat strokes.

articles.headline: At nearly 50%, no. of deaths without medical care up sharply since 2020
articles.summary: The percentage of deaths without medical certification in India has risen sharply to almost 50% in 2023, up from 30% in 2020. This indicates a significant decline in access to formal healthcare or reporting.

articles.headline: Govt expects gifts to flow freely into PM Cares Fund
articles.summary: The government expects more gifts to flow into the PM Cares Fund, especially from NRIs and foreign entities. The fund, set up for Covid-19 relief, has drawn criticism for its lack of transparency.

articles.headline: Vikram of Norway: Pragg wins crown that eluded even Vishy
articles.summary: R Praggnanandhaa defeated world champion Ding Liren at the Norway Chess tournament, securing a historic victory that even the legendary Viswanathan Anand has not achieved. This marks a significant milestone in his career.

articles.headline: 17 escape unhurt in Shivamogga
articles.summary: Seventeen people escaped unhurt after a mini-bus caught fire near Shivamogga. A short circuit is suspected to be the cause. Firefighters quickly responded, preventing any casualties.

articles.headline: Two months on, police book advocate & mother for 6-year-old’s murder
articles.summary: Bengaluru police have registered a murder case against an advocate and his mother for the death of his 6-year-old daughter two months ago. The family had previously claimed the girl fell from a tree. The girl's paternal aunt filed the complaint.

articles.headline: Different dreams, same determination: Toppers set sights beyond tech
articles.summary: Karnataka Common Entrance Test (CET) toppers are diversifying their career choices beyond traditional engineering, with many opting for medical, design, and commerce courses. Students emphasize following their passion and exploring new avenues.

articles.headline: Only few from govt colleges in top ranks
articles.summary: Very few students from government pre-university colleges secured ranks in the top 10,000 of the Karnataka Common Entrance Test (CET). This highlights a disparity in quality or access between government and private institutions.

articles.headline: High PU exam scores drive shift in ranks
articles.summary: High scores in the Second Pre-University (PU) examination have led to significant shifts in Karnataka Common Entrance Test (CET) ranks, with many students securing top positions. This reflects a growing trend of academic excellence in the state.

articles.headline: Crooks exploit exam deadline, pocket ₹1L
articles.summary: A man was swindled of ₹1 lakh by fraudsters posing as university officials, claiming he missed a deadline to submit exam fees. The culprits used forged documents and manipulated him into transferring money to multiple accounts.

articles.headline: Metro-suburban double-decker saved us ₹169cr: K-RIDE
articles.summary: K-RIDE's double-decker railway project for the Bengaluru Suburban Rail Project (BSRP) has saved ₹169 crore by optimizing land acquisition costs. The innovative design allows both metro and suburban trains to run on the same viaduct.

articles.headline: OC rule exemption for power extended to 2,400 govt buildings
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has extended the exemption from Occupancy Certificate (OC) rules for power supply to 2,400 government buildings. This aims to streamline processes and ensure essential services despite non-compliance with OC mandates.

articles.headline: Eating hair? This rare disorder is sending people to doctors
articles.summary: Doctors are reporting an increase in cases of Trichotillomania and Trichophagia, rare disorders where individuals pull out and sometimes eat their own hair. The conditions are often linked to stress, anxiety, and obsessive-compulsive tendencies, requiring specialized medical intervention.

articles.headline: Pool drownings to EV fires: Who is liable in apartments?
articles.summary: With rising incidents like pool drownings and EV fires in apartment complexes, legal experts debate liability. The Karnataka Apartment Ownership Act is yet to be implemented, leaving a grey area for accountability between associations and developers.

articles.headline: UK-based relative suspects foul play
articles.summary: The UK-based relative of a woman who died under mysterious circumstances in Bengaluru in 2018 suspects foul play. The woman's husband and his family are under scrutiny, with the relative alleging discrepancies in the investigation.

articles.headline: Crane moves backward, hits building
articles.summary: A crane operating near Whitefield, Bengaluru, moved backward and crashed into a building under construction, causing significant damage. No casualties were reported, but the incident highlights safety concerns at construction sites.

articles.headline: Owner cannot be compelled to give up land for free: HC
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that a property owner cannot be forced to surrender land for road widening without compensation. This decision reinforces property rights and limits the government's power in infrastructure projects.

articles.headline: Cycle track near tech park becomes garbage hub, locals alarmed
articles.summary: A newly constructed cycle track near Ecospace Business Park in Bengaluru has become a dumping ground for garbage, leading to health concerns and complaints from local residents. Authorities are urged to intervene and maintain cleanliness.

articles.headline: Cabinet expansion to be a tougher test for Shivakumar
articles.summary: The Karnataka Congress faces challenges in cabinet expansion, with Deputy CM DK Shivakumar needing to balance regional and caste equations while navigating demands from senior leaders and newly inducted members.

articles.headline: Faith in Bengaluru in do-or-don't era, Congress has to do it now
articles.summary: Ahead of the Congress Legislature Party meeting, party members in Bengaluru emphasize the need for immediate action and effective governance to maintain public trust. They urge leaders to resolve internal conflicts swiftly.

articles.headline: CM held kkk with DKS till 1.30am
articles.summary: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah held a late-night meeting with Deputy CM DK Shivakumar until 1:30 AM to discuss cabinet expansion and address internal party issues. The meeting aimed to iron out differences and strategy.

articles.headline: AICC chief Kharge has ₹38.7cr in assets, faces 5 pending cases
articles.summary: Congress President Mallikarjun Kharge's election affidavit reveals assets worth ₹38.7 crore and five pending criminal cases. His financial declarations provide insight into his wealth and legal standing.

articles.headline: The B'luru strongman cannot afford to ignore
articles.summary: Deputy CM DK Shivakumar, often seen as a powerful figure in Bengaluru, faces pressure to address local issues and consolidate his political base. Ignoring public sentiment and party workers could undermine his influence.

articles.headline: Implicating lawyers of opposite parties in criminal cases a dangerous trend: HC
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court condemned the trend of implicating lawyers from opposing parties in criminal cases, calling it a dangerous and unethical practice that undermines the legal system. It emphasized the need for fair legal processes.

articles.headline: HC refuses to quash case against man selling fake Aadhaar cards
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court rejected a plea to quash a case against a man accused of selling fake Aadhaar cards. The court upheld the charges, stating that the fraudulent activity impacts national security and identity verification systems.

articles.headline: New bride reveals rape as minor, man seeks justice
articles.summary: A new bride revealed she was raped as a minor, leading to her husband seeking justice against the accused. The man, who allegedly raped her, has been arrested. The incident highlights issues of child sexual abuse and justice.

articles.headline: How far a US dollar goes around the world
articles.summary: The article presents a graphic showing the purchasing power of the US dollar in various countries, indicating differences in cost of living and exchange rates globally.

articles.headline: Hyd woman dies, toddler guardian injured after fall from building
articles.summary: A woman died and her toddler was injured after falling from a building in Hyderabad. Police are investigating whether it was an accident or suicide. The child is receiving medical care.

articles.headline: AIADMK exodus swells; ex-mantris, MLAs join TVK
articles.summary: Several former AIADMK ministers and MLAs have defected to actor Vijay's Tamizhaga Vetri Kazhagam (TVK), signaling a significant exodus from the AIADMK. This strengthens TVK's political presence ahead of elections.

articles.headline: BSF ends border standoff, says illegals sent to B’desh
articles.summary: The Border Security Force (BSF) announced the end of the border standoff with Bangladesh, stating that 1,833 Bangladeshi nationals, who had been stranded in India due to lockdown, have been sent back to Bangladesh.

articles.headline: Rural-urban divide points to healthcare access gap
articles.summary: A study reveals a significant gap in healthcare access and outcomes between rural and urban areas, with rural populations facing higher mortality rates and poorer health infrastructure. The disparities highlight systemic inequalities.

articles.headline: Import Export Policy & Procedure of DEFT, Customs, RBI
articles.summary: This article details the government's import-export policy and procedures, including guidelines from the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT), Customs, and the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). It covers regulations and compliance for international trade.

articles.headline: TMC rift plays out in Delhi as rebel MPs weigh split
articles.summary: Internal rifts within the Trinamool Congress (TMC) have surfaced in Delhi, with some rebel MPs reportedly considering a split. The division highlights growing dissent and power struggles within the party ahead of upcoming elections.

articles.headline: Next few days will be crucial as competing weather systems battle for control
articles.summary: The next few days are critical for India's monsoon as competing weather systems, including a western disturbance and a low-pressure area, battle for dominance. This will determine the monsoon's progress and impact on rainfall.

articles.headline: Court describes celebrity firing 'scourge which often causes fatalities' in India
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court condemned the "scourge" of celebratory firing, which frequently results in fatalities across India. The court emphasized the need for stricter enforcement of laws to curb such dangerous practices.

articles.headline: India bloc meet to see some straight talk tomorrow
articles.summary: The India opposition bloc is expected to hold a meeting tomorrow to address internal differences and devise a unified strategy against the ruling government. Leaders anticipate direct and honest discussions on alliance coordination.

articles.headline: Tax moves aim to boost g-sec market
articles.summary: The government is implementing tax incentives and regulatory changes to boost the Government Securities (G-Sec) market, aiming to attract more investors and enhance market liquidity. This aligns with India's inclusion in the Global Bond Index.

articles.headline: 189 pesticide samples failed quality tests
articles.summary: Out of 5,500 pesticide samples tested, 189 failed quality standards, indicating widespread issues with agricultural inputs. The samples were collected from across states, raising concerns about food safety and agricultural practices.

articles.headline: Not just Gen Z, but Gen X draws people away
articles.summary: The article discusses how both Generation Z and Generation X are increasingly disengaging from traditional social and political structures, opting for more individualistic or community-focused activities. It explores the reasons behind this societal shift.

articles.headline: 9 of family killed as truck hits pickup
articles.summary: Nine members of a family were killed when a truck collided with their pickup vehicle in Uttar Pradesh. The accident, caused by rash driving, also left several others injured.

articles.headline: You can delete our posts, but you can't erase us, says Dipke
articles.summary: Activist Dipke criticized social media platforms for deleting posts related to farmers' protests, stating that while content can be removed, the spirit of dissent cannot be erased. He emphasized the importance of freedom of speech.

articles.headline: Muslim youth to 'stay away' from CJP stir
articles.summary: Muslim youth organizations have announced they will stay away from the Citizen Justice Party (CJP) stir, citing the party's perceived anti-minority stance and lack of clear agenda. This reduces the CJP's intended broad support base.

articles.headline: The steep cost of ignoring Delhi's urban villages
articles.summary: Delhi's urban villages are rapidly gentrifying without proper planning, leading to infrastructural strain, loss of cultural identity, and housing challenges. Ignoring these areas has created significant social and economic costs.

articles.headline: AI can draw on Agatha Christie, but it can't take the next level she did
articles.summary: The article argues that while AI can replicate patterns and styles, it lacks the human genius, emotional depth, and originality of writers like Agatha Christie. It emphasizes that true creativity remains beyond AI's current capabilities.

articles.headline: AI can now solve JEE papers. Can it give students a fairer shot at success?
articles.summary: IIT-Bombay's research shows AI can solve JEE papers, raising questions about its potential to revolutionize education and make competitive exams fairer. However, concerns remain about equitable access and preventing misuse.

articles.headline: Dear Indian tourist, the world is not your dance stage
articles.summary: The article criticizes the behavior of some Indian tourists abroad, specifically their tendency to treat public spaces as dance floors and disrespect local customs. It urges for more responsible and respectful tourism.

articles.headline: Me Too, You Too? Shilpa Shinde’s fake allegation revives debate
articles.summary: Shilpa Shinde's retracted "Me Too" allegation against a co-star has reignited debate on false accusations and their impact on genuine victims. It highlights the complexities of the movement and the need for due diligence.

articles.headline: Oh-zempic!: Smaller waists and bigger questions
articles.summary: The article explores the growing popularity of weight-loss drugs like Ozempic, leading to "smaller waists." However, it raises bigger questions about long-term health effects, accessibility, and societal implications of relying on medication for weight management.

articles.headline: Recipe for Doi Chire (Dahi Chivda)
articles.summary: This article provides a recipe for Doi Chire (Dahi Chivda), a traditional Bengali breakfast or snack made with flattened rice and yogurt, offering a quick and healthy meal option.

articles.headline: Cry clubs pop up in Bengaluru as people find comfort in collective emotional release
articles.summary: "Cry clubs" are emerging in Bengaluru, offering spaces for collective emotional release and mental well-being. These groups provide a non-judgmental environment for individuals to express their feelings and connect with others.

articles.headline: O pushpa, I love tears! Indians are getting together to have a good cry
articles.summary: Indians are increasingly embracing "cry clubs" and shared emotional release, moving away from traditional stoicism. These spaces provide an outlet for stress and foster connection, reflecting a cultural shift towards emotional openness.

articles.headline: ‘Be it C-section or natural birth, no mother is taking an easy route’
articles.summary: Actress Surveen Chawla discusses the pressures and judgments surrounding C-sections versus natural births. She emphasizes that both paths involve immense effort and sacrifice, urging for empathy and respect towards mothers' choices.

articles.headline: How NEET parents can help children 'peak' again
articles.summary: The article provides advice for parents on how to support their children preparing for the NEET exam, focusing on strategies for managing stress, maintaining motivation, and creating a conducive study environment for optimal performance.

articles.headline: India and the World
articles.summary: The article discusses the global impact of Indian-origin football players, highlighting their presence in various international leagues and national teams, reflecting India's growing influence in world football.

articles.headline: Silent Epidemic: 1 in 3 Indian adults have high blood pressure
articles.summary: A significant number of Indian adults, 1 in 3, suffer from high blood pressure, leading to a "silent epidemic." This alarming statistic highlights a major public health crisis requiring widespread awareness and intervention.

articles.headline: Buckets Nailed Down
articles.summary: The article features Rohit Chandran, a footballer from Kerala who moved to Malaysia and has been instrumental in his team's success. He aims to inspire more Indian players to pursue careers abroad.

articles.headline: Those who missed the flight
articles.summary: This section briefly highlights Indian-origin footballers who, despite their talent, did not make it to major international teams, discussing their contributions to smaller leagues and the challenges they faced.

articles.headline: Iraq striker Hussein questioned for hours at Chicago airport
articles.summary: Iraq's national football team striker Ali Hussein was questioned for several hours at Chicago airport, causing delays for the team. The incident raised concerns about travel protocols and player treatment.

articles.headline: Shreyas Yadav is standout candidate
articles.summary: Shreyas Yadav is highlighted as a standout candidate for an Indian cricket team spot, showcasing impressive recent performances and consistent form in domestic and international matches.

articles.headline: He'll do a great job as India skipper: Ponting
articles.summary: Ricky Ponting has endorsed Shreyas Iyer as a strong candidate for India's captaincy, praising his leadership qualities and performance. This comes amidst discussions about future leadership roles in Indian cricket.

articles.headline: Antonelli takes pole
articles.summary: Andrea Antonelli secured pole position for the upcoming MotoGP race, demonstrating strong pace in qualifying. This performance positions him favorably for the race.

articles.headline: India claim sixth SAFF Women's Championship
articles.summary: India's women's football team won their sixth SAFF Women's Championship title, defeating Bangladesh in the final. This victory solidifies their dominance in regional football.

articles.headline: Exceptional Pragg shines in elite field
articles.summary: R Praggnanandhaa delivered an exceptional performance in an elite chess tournament, outshining seasoned grandmasters. His strategic brilliance and composure earned him significant praise from the chess community.

articles.headline: Zverev, Cobolli bid to end Sinner wait
articles.summary: Alexander Zverev and Flavio Cobolli are set to face off in a tennis match, with both players aiming to end Jannik Sinner's long wait for a major title by disrupting the tournament draw.

articles.headline: Xanthe clinches stunning victory
articles.summary: Xanthe, ridden by Jockey Akshay Kumar, secured a stunning victory in the feature race at the Bengaluru horse races. The win was a surprise for many, showcasing an impressive performance.

articles.headline: Zuccaro fancied for main event
articles.summary: Zuccaro is a strong contender and highly favored to win the main event at the Bengaluru races. Expert predictions indicate a high probability of success for the horse.

articles.headline: Prathyusha stars for Palar
articles.summary: Prathyusha delivered a stellar performance for Palar, leading her team to victory in a local sports event. Her contributions were crucial for the team's success.

articles.headline: US company benefits from Chief of Staff Stewart
articles.summary: A US company allegedly benefited from its close ties with Mark Stewart, Chief of Staff to the Vice President, securing contracts and favorable treatment. This raises concerns about potential conflicts of interest and influence peddling.

articles.headline: US attacks Iran radar sites as drones fly towards Hormuz
articles.summary: US forces attacked Iranian radar sites following drone strikes towards the Strait of Hormuz. This escalation increases tensions in the Middle East, with both sides on high alert.

articles.headline: Trump, Sanders & Altman moot public ownership in AI
articles.summary: Donald Trump, Bernie Sanders, and Sam Altman have all expressed interest in public ownership models for AI, though for different reasons. This signals a growing debate on how to regulate and control powerful AI technologies.

articles.headline: Vance blames UK student's murder on migrant, 'too much invasion'
articles.summary: US Senator J.D. Vance blamed the murder of a UK student on a migrant, linking it to broader concerns about "too much invasion" of illegal immigrants. This statement has sparked controversy and criticism.

articles.headline: Israel strike in Gaza kills 7, month-old baby
articles.summary: An Israeli airstrike in Gaza killed seven people, including a month-old baby, during ongoing hostilities. The attack has drawn international condemnation amid calls for de-escalation in the region.

articles.headline: Drone barrage hits St. Petersburg early
articles.summary: A drone barrage targeted St. Petersburg, causing explosions and damage in the early hours. Authorities are investigating the source and extent of the attack, which highlights ongoing regional tensions.

articles.headline: Sriram Krishnan, Trump's AI brain, to exit White House
articles.summary: Sriram Krishnan, a key advisor to Donald Trump on artificial intelligence, is reportedly leaving the White House. His departure comes amidst ongoing policy discussions and the development of AI strategies.

articles.headline: Jensen charm suited & streets
articles.summary: Danish actor Johannes Jensen, known for his unique charm, has been capturing audiences both on screen and through his public persona. The article discusses his rise to fame and connection with fans.

articles.headline: $176,000 for an NBA ticket: Why watching sports is now a luxury
articles.summary: The article discusses the escalating cost of sporting event tickets, with some NBA tickets reaching $176,000, making live sports a luxury accessible only to the ultra-wealthy. This trend raises concerns about exclusivity.

articles.headline: Taipei welcomes a new panda cub
articles.summary: Taipei Zoo has welcomed a new panda cub, delighting visitors and conservationists. The birth marks a success for their breeding program and contributes to panda conservation efforts.

articles.headline: The Daily Planet
articles.summary: A couple tied the knot underwater in a unique wedding ceremony in the Maldives, exchanging vows amidst marine life. The event celebrated their love for the ocean and drew attention to marine conservation.

articles.headline: PM Modi reviews impact of West Asia crisis on Indian economy
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a meeting of the Economic Advisory Council to assess the impact of the West Asia conflict on India's economy. The meeting followed robust FY26 growth data showing 7.7% real GDP growth. Discussions focused on sustaining growth momentum, improving Ease of Doing Business, and implementing reforms to ensure Ease of Living and boost domestic consumption.

articles.headline: India announces foreign investment reforms to stabilize capital account
articles.summary: India's new foreign investment reforms aim to provide capital account stability, strengthen the rupee, and improve liquidity. Measures include tax concessions for government bonds to attract foreign investors and portfolio managers. This follows strong GDP growth (7.7% in 2025-26) and appreciating rupee.

articles.headline: Shreyas Iyer named India's new T20 captain, Suryakumar Yadav dropped
articles.summary: Shreyas Iyer replaces Suryakumar Yadav as India's T20 captain for the England and Ireland tour. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, 15, earns his first call-up, potentially becoming the youngest debutant. Jasprit Bumrah is rested for the T20 series but named for the Asian Games squad.

articles.headline: Domestic LPG price hiked by ₹29 per cylinder in Delhi
articles.summary: The price of domestic LPG cylinders in Delhi has increased by ₹29, reaching ₹942 from ₹913, effective Sunday. This is the second price hike in three months, attributed to elevated global energy costs affecting state-owned fuel retailers.

articles.headline: Will induct Zorawar light tank in 2028-29: Army chief
articles.summary: The Indian Army expects to induct the Zorawar light tank in 2028-29 after fixing development issues, boosting mountain deployment. Army chief General Upendra Dwivedi highlighted the tank's capabilities and the importance of indigenous defense solutions.

articles.headline: Mirra Andreeva wins 1st French Open title at 19
articles.summary: Mirra Andreeva, 19, became the youngest French Open women's singles champion in over three decades by defeating Maja Chwalinska 6-3, 6-2. She fulfilled a prophecy of becoming a Grand Slam champion.

articles.headline: AIADMK leaders join TVK, citing lack of respect
articles.summary: Four former AIADMK ministers and six former MLAs joined the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), citing lack of respect and discipline within AIADMK. They praised TVK's transparent governance and family atmosphere.

articles.headline: UP cops more loyal to govt than Constitution, says HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court criticized Uttar Pradesh police, stating they appear more loyal to the government than to the Constitution. The court made this observation during a hearing related to a case of alleged police excesses.

articles.headline: KL Rahul, Shubman Gill hit tons as India dominate Afghanistan
articles.summary: KL Rahul (100) and Shubman Gill (103*) scored centuries as India reached 368/3 on Day 1 against Afghanistan in the one-off Test. Rishabh Pant (50*) also contributed, showcasing India's batting dominance.

articles.headline: CBSE students wait for marks, grace marks
articles.summary: CBSE class 10 and 12 students are anxiously awaiting results, with many hoping for grace marks due to errors in question papers and evaluation issues. Parents' associations have urged the board to be lenient given the lockdown circumstances.
